defects.summary: Money aggregated as IEEE-754 float across Fund; totals can drift by a cent
defects.reproduction: Sum [0.1, 0.2] via reduce(+) -> 0.30000000000000004; roll up N balances
defects.root_cause: No deterministic money primitive; ad-hoc float arithmetic
defects.fix: Added src/lib/fund/money.ts (integer minor units + exact sumMoney/sumBy); routed safeMoney through it
defects.verification: All listed aggregation sites now route through sumBy/sumMoney; net-worth combinations use sumMoney; 427 tests green, tsc clean
defects.similar_code_search: All reduce(+ Number()) money aggregation sites in Fund adopted in wave 4.2 (OverviewModule, FundNetWorthModule, FundLiabilities, FundRecurringList, FundCashflowModule, financeDailyJobs, usePlaidConnection)

defects.summary: safeMoney accepted sub-cent floats without rounding to the currency minor unit
defects.root_cause: Raw Number() parse
defects.fix: safeMoney delegates to parseMoney (cent-rounded)
defects.verification: existing financeNarratorContext.test.ts still passes

defects.summary: Make webhook delivery treated every HTTP response as success
defects.reproduction: Return HTTP 503 from an env-configured Make webhook; the previous triggerWebhook resolved and notifyViaMake wrote result=success
defects.root_cause: The outbound helper parsed response text without checking Response.ok and exposed no typed delivery contract
defects.fix: Return the shared Result<MakeWebhookReceipt>, accept only 2xx, disable redirects, and write normalized failure audit metadata
defects.verification: HTTP 503 maps to provider_error, no success audit is written, focused tests and full 737-test suite pass

defects.summary: Raw Make response/error content could enter exceptions and persistent audit metadata
defects.reproduction: Return a provider body containing a token-like value or throw an error containing the opaque hook path; previous code embedded raw text in MakeError/audit_logs
defects.root_cause: Provider response bodies and thrown messages were treated as safe observability content
defects.fix: Never read webhook response bodies; emit generic structured errors and persist only code/status/retryability/idempotency metadata
defects.verification: Secret-bearing response/error fixtures are absent from Results, Sentry messages, and audit rows; independent review found no substantiated regression

defects.summary: Failed Make writes had no durable replay state and duplicate prevention depended entirely on each remote scenario
defects.reproduction: Return a provider failure or lose the response after Make accepts a write; the prior implementation retained only audit metadata and could not safely claim or replay the payload
defects.root_cause: No durable encrypted write outbox, local dedupe record, or compare-and-swap replay claim
defects.fix: Persist purpose-separated AES-GCM payload envelopes behind metadata-only owner RLS, hash dedupe keys, claim attempts atomically, dead-letter bounded failures, and allow only explicit authenticated operator replay
defects.verification: 17 focused tests pass; local and hosted migrations verify RLS/grants; duplicate, tamper, failed-delivery, third-attempt dead-letter, and replay-success paths are covered

defects.source: Hosted Supabase security advisor after wave 10.14 DDL
defects.summary: Anonymous and authenticated Data API roles could execute the SECURITY DEFINER expire_stale_approvals RPC across all users
defects.reproduction: has_function_privilege returned true and the advisor reported public/authenticated SECURITY DEFINER execution
defects.root_cause: The cron used a browser-facing Supabase client and the RPC retained default PUBLIC plus authenticated EXECUTE grants
defects.fix: Run maintenance RPCs with the service-role client and revoke EXECUTE from PUBLIC, anon, and authenticated while granting service_role only
defects.verification: Local and hosted has_function_privilege: anon=false, authenticated=false, service_role=true; security advisor warning cleared

defects.source: Phase 10 Make management API review plus current official scenario API reference
defects.summary: Make management helpers threw unstructured errors, trusted unvalidated provider data, retried no reads, accepted invalid configuration, and sent scenario inputs under the wrong field
defects.reproduction: Call runScenario with input and inspect the old request body: it used `input`, while Make API v2 requires `data`; malformed team ids became NaN and every management failure escaped as MakeError
defects.root_cause: The older management client predated the shared Result contract, runtime response schemas, provider timing policy, and the current Make scenario-input API
defects.fix: Return schema-validated Result values for list/detail/run/start/stop; validate configuration and ids; reject redirects; retry reads at most three times; attempt writes once; use `{responsive:true,data}`; require id/state activation confirmation; emit only normalized safe failures
defects.verification: 49 focused tests pass; 757-test full suite passes; configured Make list/detail reads passed live id/team/state shape checks without exposing scenario content; no live write performed

defects.source: Platform audit L2/I4 and Phase 10 Composio cache synchronization review
defects.summary: Mail first paint blocked on live provider fan-out and a failed account could not retain durable last-known inbox state
defects.reproduction: Open /mail with multiple direct or Composio accounts while one provider is slow or unavailable; the prior GET /api/mail/inbox waited on every provider and had no persistent rows to render offline
defects.root_cause: Inbox list metadata had no owner-scoped cache or per-account sync state; provider refresh and first paint were the same request
defects.fix: Serve normalized header/snippet metadata from owner-RLS cache, revalidate explicitly through POST /api/mail/sync, retain failed-account rows during partial refresh, record normalized sync outcomes, and keep message bodies live-only
defects.verification: Focused Mail/cache/sync/disconnect tests pass; 768-test full suite and seeded authenticated Mail smoke pass; local and hosted migration/RLS/owner-isolation checks pass

defects.summary: Concentration routine requests accepted malformed JSON shapes and silently replaced explicit unsafe thresholds with a default
defects.reproduction: POST JSON null or an explicit maxWeight below zero or above one to /api/routines/concentration-check; the previous unvalidated body access could destabilize error handling or continue with 25 percent without telling the caller
defects.root_cause: The route cast arbitrary JSON to a request type and treated validation failure as absence instead of distinguishing omitted from invalid input
defects.fix: Parse a strict Zod request contract, require finite maxWeight in (0, 1], require UUID run ids, and return visible INVALID_ROUTINE_INPUT 400 responses before any database or routine work
defects.verification: Malformed null and unsafe threshold cases return 400 with no profile query or routine execution; valid request/profile/default provenance cases remain covered

defects.summary: The deterministic signal severity model was not consumed by Dispatch and did not recognize the application's persisted hi priority vocabulary
defects.reproduction: Persist an action signal with metadata.ai_priority=hi; the prior UI grouped it as Action and deriveSeverity returned actionable instead of critical. Archive a title and resurface a punctuation variant; the queue did not use that resolution history to demote it.
defects.root_cause: Phase 7 shipped pure severity and normalized dedup primitives without wiring them into the existing Dispatch filters/groups, and its urgent label set used high but not the repository's hi enum
defects.fix: Recognize hi as urgent, derive every queue row's tier from persisted type/priority, group and filter by the four Need-to-Know tiers, and feed resolved routed/dismissed/archived titles into normalized duplicate detection while excluding the current record
defects.verification: Focused tests cover hi escalation and punctuation-varied resolution memory; synthetic local desktop/mobile verification showed one row in each tier and responsive filter wrapping

defects.summary: Root-scoped semantic font aliases ignored body-level next/font and Interface Studio runtime choices
defects.reproduction: Set --disp or --sans on body and inspect an element consuming --type-display-family or --type-body-family; the root-resolved alias retained the earlier stack.
defects.root_cause: Semantic aliases were declared on :root while runtime font variables are intentionally set on body.
defects.fix: Bind semantic family aliases on body alongside the live font variables and retain size/leading roles at :root.
defects.verification: Static contract rejects root family aliases and the authenticated gallery verifies a body-level runtime face change through computed style.

defects.summary: Light primary controls, accent-derived focus rings, and Slate/Light faint microtext failed required contrast
defects.reproduction: Compute WCAG ratios for Light --on-accent against each selectable accent, accent-bright focus against Light canvas, and --ink-faint against Slate/Light surfaces; multiple combinations fall below 4.5:1 text or 3:1 UI contrast.
defects.root_cause: Interaction foreground/focus colors inherited decorative accent choices, and faint text values were chosen visually against canvas only.
defects.fix: Use stable near-black foreground on every selectable accent, derive focus from theme ink, and raise each theme's faint ink to AA across all solid surfaces.
defects.verification: Automated matrix covers all four themes, four solid surfaces per theme, and all six accent plus hover pairs.

defects.summary: TSX accessibility tests were undiscoverable and shared segmented controls exposed repeated generic group names
defects.reproduction: Run Vitest against designSystemA11y.test.tsx under the former include pattern and inspect Tasks/Approvals/Memory/Interface Studio Seg groups in the accessibility tree.
defects.root_cause: Vitest included only .test.ts, while Seg supplied a default 'Options' label instead of requiring caller context.
defects.fix: Discover .test.tsx, require ariaLabel or aria-labelledby at the Seg type boundary, and name every production consumer.
defects.verification: Both focused TSX and token files execute (16 tests), all call sites typecheck, and the authenticated gallery exposes named theme/density groups.

defects.summary: Late or failed remote preference hydration could overwrite a user edit, trigger a blind default upsert, discard timezone/future fields, and persist writes out of order
defects.root_cause: Remote writes were enabled before a successful read, hydration ignored concurrent local edits, the JSON envelope was replaced wholesale, and debounced writes were not serialized
defects.fix: Enable sync only after a successful read, guard remote fields with edit epochs, preserve the loaded envelope and timezone, parse metadata separately from interface settings, and serialize remote upserts
defects.verification: Pure envelope/ordering tests pass; deterministic delayed-read and failed-read browser tests pass; full authenticated production-mode suite passes 13/13

defects.summary: Task and approval lifecycle routes use stale reads followed by unconditional updates, and task activity failures can be hidden
defects.root_cause: Lifecycle validation and mutation are separate requests without compare-and-swap or a transaction
defects.fix: Route lifecycle changes through owner-derived SECURITY DEFINER RPCs that require the expected status and lifecycle version, update the row with compare-and-swap semantics, and append task activity or approval audit in the same transaction. Enforce stored approval scope, challenge id, expiry, action class, and financial freshness server-side. Stage a separate contract migration that revokes legacy direct client mutation only after compatible code is live.
defects.verification: Focused route/domain tests and the local task/approval SQL validator pass concurrent stale-version rejection, atomic activity/audit failure rollback, ownership, strict scope/challenge, expiry/freshness, and successful transition checks. Hosted expansion migration 202607161300 is applied and read back; contract migration 202607161401 remains intentionally pending until the compatible application revision is production-live.
defects.similar_code_search: The same stale-read/unconditional-update pattern was reviewed across task transitions, approval decisions/step-up/execution, WebAuthn counters, and routine resume. WebAuthn and routine resume received their own atomic RPC contracts in the same revision.

defects.summary: Routine resume can consume approval before successful resumed work and lacks a complete atomic claim/retry contract
defects.root_cause: Approval consumption, run claim, pause metadata, execution, and finalization are not coordinated as a retry-safe state transition
defects.fix: Claim resumable runs through an owner-derived lease and fencing-token RPC, replay completed step outputs, materialize resumed tasks with stable idempotency keys, renew/release only with the active fence, and atomically finalize the run while consuming the terminal approval. Failed work releases or expires the claim without consuming approval, allowing a safe retry.
defects.verification: Focused route/executor/claim tests and the 50-check local SQL validator pass double-submit exclusion, stale-fence rejection, lease expiry and reclaim, failed-resume retry, idempotent task materialization, replayed completed outputs, finalization-failure rollback, and atomic terminal approval consumption. Hosted expansion migration 202607161400 is applied and read back.

defects.source: Phase 9 financial-safety and routine-version adversarial review
defects.summary: Changing the rebalance proposal in place could reinterpret persisted v1 runs with new simulation semantics, while approval/broker placeholders made a non-executing workflow appear closer to financial execution than it was
defects.reproduction: Resolve a historical rebalance-proposal v1 run after replacing the registered definition in place, or run the previous route and observe approval/waiting constructs despite no authorized broker execution path
defects.root_cause: The routine behavior changed without an immutable version boundary, and the proposal path mixed simulation output with approval/execution-shaped state
defects.fix: Restore the historical approval-producing v1 definition for persisted-run interpretation, register a new v2 definition, and stamp all new route runs as v2. Make v2 strictly simulation-only: create no approvals, broker placeholders, or waiting state; enforce bounded inputs/rates and a complete positive USD fresh-or-delayed quote set; fail closed on missing/unsafe data; emit only complete explicitly unsubmitted order drafts.
defects.verification: Focused route/versioning tests cover historical v1 resolution, v2 stamping, no approval creation, no broker placeholder/waiting state, rate and request limits, quote completeness/currency/freshness rejection, blocked-run persistence, and complete unsubmitted drafts. Full 1064-test suite, tsc, lint, and independent financial-safety review are green.

defects.summary: Model-authored action paths are navigated without an internal allowlist and heuristic fallbacks can be indistinguishable from model success
defects.root_cause: Untrusted model output crosses the navigation boundary and provider degradation is encoded as a normal success payload
defects.fix: Normalize model actions through a strict internal-route allowlist before rendering or navigation, reject external/protocol-relative/unknown paths, and attach explicit provider/source/degraded provenance to heuristic or unavailable-provider fallbacks. Keep observability metadata bounded and content-free.
defects.verification: 72 focused AI boundary tests pass, including unsafe action paths, normalized internal paths, provider failure/degraded provenance, heuristic fallback labeling, and privacy-safe telemetry. Current full suite passes 1064 tests across 165 files; tsc and lint are clean.
defects.similar_code_search: Console and mascot action consumers now share the normalized contract; provider fallbacks returned by the AI route are discriminated from model success instead of relying on display text.

defects.summary: An enrolled TOTP factor could be bypassed after passkey login because the restored Supabase session was AAL1 and MFA enforcement existed only in client login code
defects.reproduction: Complete passkey verification for a user with an enrolled TOTP factor, retain the server-issued magic-link cookie, then call a protected API directly without running the login page's client-side assurance check
defects.root_cause: Passkey possession restored a valid Supabase cookie, but middleware and API entry points checked only getUser(); the AAL1-to-AAL2 requirement was initiated exclusively by client-side login code
defects.fix: Evaluate authenticator assurance in middleware for every authenticated protected request. Deny protected APIs with MFA_REQUIRED, redirect protected pages to the login ceremony, allow only the exact MFA challenge/verify bootstrap routes, keep other MFA management blocked, and fail closed when assurance is unavailable. The login page resumes the server-directed MFA ceremony without redirect loops.
defects.verification: Eight focused assurance/middleware tests prove direct protected API denial at aal1, exact challenge/verify access, login recovery, and protected access at aal2. The full 1064-test suite, tsc, and lint pass. A real hardware authenticator plus TOTP ceremony remains an explicit human gate.
defects.similar_code_search: All middleware-protected and route-owned APIs now pass through the same assurance boundary; unenroll and other MFA management routes are intentionally not bootstrap exceptions.

defects.summary: Fresh-stack authenticated CI could undo the final passkey privilege contract by converting a surviving FOR ALL policy back into browser insert/update/delete grants
defects.reproduction: Apply migration 202607161401, then run scripts/sql/bootstrap-local-e2e-role-grants.sql; the former policy-derived grant loop mapped the legacy Users manage own passkeys policy to full DML, contradicting the contract revokes
defects.root_cause: The contract revoked table privileges without replacing the older FOR ALL passkey RLS policy, while the local CI bootstrap assumed every policy command should regenerate matching table privileges
defects.fix: The pending contract migration drops the legacy FOR ALL policy and creates owner-scoped SELECT only. The CI bootstrap reasserts lifecycle DML revocations after policy-derived grants, and CI immediately runs the contract verifier. The verifier checks policy/grant catalogs and executes authenticated passkey insert/update/delete attempts that must fail at the privilege boundary.
defects.verification: Updated migration, CI-equivalent grant bootstrap, catalog verifier, and real authenticated insert/update/delete denial probes pass against local Supabase. release:validate records contract checksum 31291dd9e3a0d9eb5aac3766015036becaebff005d417f8bf16ad3fbd37b78f7. Hosted migration 202607161401 remains intentionally unapplied until the compatible production SHA is live.
defects.similar_code_search: The same post-bootstrap revocation covers agent_tasks, agent_task_activity, approvals, and user_passkeys so a future broad policy cannot silently restore browser lifecycle writes.

defects.summary: The authenticated CI bootstrap could not execute valid multi-statement SQL through Supabase's prepared-query file path
defects.reproduction: Run the fresh-stack authenticated job with `supabase db query --local --file scripts/sql/bootstrap-local-e2e-role-grants.sql`; PostgreSQL rejects the script with `cannot insert multiple commands into a prepared statement`
defects.root_cause: The CLI query path submitted a multi-command SQL file as one prepared statement instead of using a script-capable PostgreSQL executor
defects.fix: Pipe the bootstrap and verifier files into the local database container and execute them with `psql -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -f -`; document the same multi-statement requirement for controlled hosted execution.
defects.verification: The exact-head clean-runner verify, public, and authenticated jobs passed in GitHub run 29528783685. The current full local suite passes 1072 tests across 166 files; typecheck, lint, release validation, production build, public 13/13, and authenticated 14/14 are green.
defects.similar_code_search: Release documentation now requires a multi-statement-capable PostgreSQL executor for every expansion, contract, and read-back SQL script instead of recommending the incompatible prepared-query path.

defects.summary: Unauthenticated same-app redirects on a preview deployment escaped to the configured production origin
defects.reproduction: Request `/command` on PR #195's protected preview and inspect the 307 Location header; the prior implementation redirected to the production alias because `buildAppUrl` reused the canonical OAuth origin
defects.root_cause: One origin helper conflated provider-registered canonical OAuth callback URLs with request-local application redirects
defects.fix: Keep `getAppOrigin` canonical for OAuth redirect URIs, but build same-app redirects from the actual request origin. Preserve exact loopback hosts for local flows and validate loopback Host syntax and port bounds before trusting it.
defects.verification: Fifteen focused origin/middleware tests cover preview isolation, no-config loopback handling, and crafted Host rejection. The current full local suite and both production-mode browser projects are green. Exact new-preview redirect validation remains required before merge.
defects.similar_code_search: All same-app redirects using `buildAppUrl`, including middleware and OAuth completion/error surfaces, now remain on the current deployment; provider callback URI construction continues to use the configured canonical helper.
